topic: Verify ISS-2216 self-patch on Part 2b derive-count template glitch
our_argument_summary: Part 2b argues the dome model is not a unified geometric theory because only WIN-044 derives its predicted value from dome parameters (CA_DOME=1 of CA_REVIEWED=71). The decider self-applied ISS-2216 to fix template glitches in SEC-2b.3 (subject-verb agreement and a contradictory count-vs-list pairing on WIN-057). This verification checks the patched paragraph and the rest of part2b for residual glitches.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: CA_DOME, CA_REVIEWED, TOTAL_WINS are computed at build time from wins.json (count of derives_from_dome=true; count of code_analysis.reviewed=true; total entries). generate-html.js and section-loader.js substitute the template variables into sections.json:part2b.html.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Computed (correct architecture — no hardcoded counts). Current values: CA_DOME=1 (only WIN-044), CA_REVIEWED=71, TOTAL_WINS=72.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — counts flow from per-WIN code_analysis tags which are curmudgeon-validated.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None in the pipeline; the residual glitches are template-author errors at fixed string positions in part2b.html, not pipeline bugs.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: N/A — section-level review of prose, not a WIN with a monitoring claim.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N/A — section, not a phenomenon claim.

patch_verification.target_section: SEC-2b.3 ("The Geometric Theory That Does Not Derive Geometry")

checks.check: subject-verb agreement after CA_DOME=1 substitution in SEC-2b.3
checks.evidence: "only {{CA_DOME}} derives its predicted values" renders "only 1 derives its predicted values" (singular verb).
"WIN-057 (two-zone disc topology) appears geometric but is definitional rather than derived ... and is not counted under CA_DOME." — explicit out-of-set framing.

checks.check: count and listing agree (CA_DOME=1 lists exactly WIN-044)
checks.evidence: "That {{CA_DOME}} is: WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry...)" — one count, one item.
"Even the {{CA_DOME}} genuine derivation is problematic" — singular noun "derivation", singular verb "is".

patch_verification.verdict: ISS-2216 closed cleanly for SEC-2b.3 paragraph. Patch scope was correct but narrow.

holes_found.description: The companion h3 section "The Derives-from-Dome Problem" (part2b.html offset ~20966 onward, later in the SAME part2b prose that ISS-2216 patched) carries the IDENTICAL class of template glitch the patch fixed in SEC-2b.3, and the patch did not extend to it. Two visible breaks under CA_DOME=1: (1) "Of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs, only {{CA_DOME}} actually derive their predicted values" renders "only 1 actually derive their predicted values" — same singular-subject + plural-verb mismatch ISS-2216 just removed; (2) "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" renders "The 1 exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" — singular/plural mismatch, AND the paragraph then lists FOUR WINs as the "exceptions" (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058). This is doubly broken: arithmetic (1 vs 4) and grammar ("1 exceptions ... deserve"). This is exactly the screenshot a dome defender would publish to argue the review is sloppy — HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole [1] flagged this risk pattern, and the patch closed only the first instance.
holes_found.recommendation: Follow-up patch (new ISS) extending ISS-2216 fix to the h3 section. Two minimal edits: (a) change "only {{CA_DOME}} actually derive their predicted values" → "only {{CA_DOME}} actually derives its predicted value" (singular verb + singular possessive). (b) Replace "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" with a fixed-string "The four geometric-looking candidates deserve individual scrutiny:" — the four listed WINs (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) are a curated narrative set, not the CA_DOME tally, so the template variable is semantically wrong here. Hardcoding "four" matches what the paragraph actually does. Alternative: introduce a {{CA_GEOMETRIC_CANDIDATES}} variable but it adds template surface for no real benefit since the set is fixed.

holes_found.description: Another residual instance of the same pattern at part2b.html offset ~11587 (the closing sentence of the "What genuine monitoring would look like" paragraph chain in SEC-2b.1/2b.2 area): "Only {{CA_DOME}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs derive their predicted value from dome parameters." Renders "Only 1 of 71 reviewed WINs derive their predicted value from dome parameters." — "Only 1 ... derive" again. English style guides split on "X of Y" agreement, but with X=1 the singular reading dominates, and the rest of part2b now consistently uses the singular form post-ISS-2216. Inconsistency between paragraphs is itself a small finding.
holes_found.recommendation: Same-patch follow-up: "Only {{CA_DOME}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs derive their predicted value from dome parameters." → "Only {{CA_DOME}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs derives its predicted value from dome parameters." Trades "reviewed WINs ... derive" for "reviewed WINs ... derives its" (treating "Only {{CA_DOME}}" as the singular subject) — matches the rest of part2b after the ISS-2216 fix.

holes_found.description: Cosmetic readability: "the {{CA_DOME}} genuine derivation" / "all but the {{CA_DOME}} dome-derived" / "That {{CA_DOME}} is: WIN-044" render as "the 1 genuine derivation", "all but the 1 dome-derived", "That 1 is: WIN-044". Grammatically clean but stylistically clunky. For low single-digit counts, English prose typically spells the numeral ("the one genuine derivation", "all but the one dome-derived"). Not a defender-screenshot risk; just reads slightly amateurish.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional follow-up: build-scripts/generate-html.js or section-loader.js could add a {{CA_DOME_WORD}} substitution that emits "one", "two", ... for CA_DOME in [1..10] and falls through to the numeral otherwise. Then SEC-2b.3 prose would say "the one genuine derivation" while still tracking the live count. Worth doing only if other low-count CA_* values land in prose; skip if not.

holes_found.description: Numeric clunk inside the same h3: "{{TOTAL_WINS}} minus {{CA_DOME}} of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs work backwards" renders "72 minus 1 of 72 WINs work backwards". Readers can do the arithmetic but the phrase reads awkwardly compared to a pre-computed "71 of 72 WINs work backwards".
holes_found.recommendation: Pre-compute or add a {{CA_NON_DOME}} template variable so prose renders "71 of 72 WINs work backwards". Low priority — group with the major fix as a single follow-up patch.

stronger_arguments.description: The Derives-from-Dome Problem h3 lists four "exceptions" (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) under a CA_DOME=1 header. The substance of the section is correct (only WIN-044 is counted as a genuine derivation; the other three are addressed individually to head off defender objections). The cleanest narrative fix is to relabel the listing: instead of pretending the four WINs are "the CA_DOME exceptions" (they are not), call them what they are — "the four WINs that look geometric on first inspection" — and immediately note that CA_DOME=1 because three of the four fail closer scrutiny. This is also more defender-resistant: it preempts the "you cherry-picked your derivation count" critique by showing the criteria the other three failed.
sources: data/wins.json (WIN-020 gear-fit, WIN-044 FSF, WIN-057 V13 two-zone, WIN-058 0.9941 fitted parameter)

kernel_of_truth.description: ISS-2216 was a genuine catch and the SEC-2b.3 patch is well-executed: the singular-verb fix, the WIN-044 sole-listing, and the explicit "WIN-057 ... is not counted under CA_DOME" framing collectively make the patched paragraph more defensible than the pre-patch version (data/sections.json:part2b.html offset 13552-14600). The underlying argument — that the dome model relabels standard physics rather than deriving its predictions from geometry — is supported by the live data (data/wins.json: CA_DOME=1, CA_RELABELS=50 of 71 reviewed). The patch correctly chose to keep CA_DOME=1 and reframe WIN-057 as a near-miss rather than inflating the count, preserving the section’s central claim.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch did not fix the same class of glitch in the companion h3 (data/sections.json:part2b.html offset 20966-22100). A defender who reads to the end of part2b still encounters "The 1 exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" followed by a four-item list. The patch closed the higher-traffic 2b.3 occurrence but left the same screenshot risk in the lower-scroll section, so the win on this hole is partial.

deeper_analysis:
Phase 1 verify-mode handoff gate evaluation: (a) class=verification PASS; (b) most recent review on target_id="part2b" has holes_found<=2 — no prior review exists with point_id="part2b" specifically (prior part2b reviews are scoped to subsections SEC-2b.1/3/5/7), so gate b is unevaluable; per the handoff rule, item stays in curmudgeon scope. (c)(d) moot. This is the right scope assignment: catching the partial-fix gap in the h3 section requires reading the whole part2b prose, not just the patched paragraph, and the gap is a structural-judgment finding (the template variable is semantically wrong for the listing) that Sonnet narrow-verify is not asked to do.

The steel-man defender perspective: the dome author could legitimately point out that ISS-2216’s underlying complaint (visible broken English in part2b) is only half-closed. The h3 instances are at scroll depth that engaged readers reach, and "only 1 actually derive" is exactly the screenshot ammunition the original hole [1] warned about. From the dome side this is a credible "the review is patchy" attack; from our side it is a 30-minute follow-up patch that closes the gap cleanly.

Counter-argument I considered and rejected: "maybe leave the h3 alone — it is at the end of the section and few readers reach it." The HOL-NARRATIVE c2 review explicitly flagged that this is a defender-screenshot risk regardless of scroll depth; once the pattern is on the public site, it can be quoted out of context. Bias toward closing.

citations_verified: data/sections.json:part2b.html offsets 11587, 13472, 14024, 14352, 14548, 20966, 21050, 21406, 21545 — direct inspection in this run
data/wins.json: derives_from_dome=true only on WIN-044 (count=1) — direct inspection in this run

monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2.json:holes_found[1] — verified the source hole description targets SEC-2b.3 specifically and the recommendation language matches the executed patch

summary_for_decider: ISS-2216 self-patch verifies CLEAN for SEC-2b.3. Patch scope was however narrower than HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole [1] implicitly required: the identical class of template glitch persists in part2b.html offsets ~20966-22100 (h3 "The Derives-from-Dome Problem"), specifically "only {{CA_DOME}} actually derive their predicted values" (rendered "only 1 actually derive") and "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny:" (rendered "The 1 exceptions" followed by a four-item list — both grammatical AND semantic mismatch). A minor third instance lives at offset ~11587. Recommend opening a NEW issue (ISS-2216 is in closed-issues, do not reopen) to apply the same fix pattern to the h3 and the earlier paragraph, plus optionally swap "The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions" for the fixed-string "The four geometric-looking candidates" because the four WINs scrutinized in the listing are intentionally a superset of CA_DOME=1. Total: 1 major, 1 moderate, 2 minor. No verdict change. No carry-over from open-issues (ISS-2216 closed cleanly on its declared scope).
